section_title: INTRODUCTION AND MENTORSHIP OVERVIEW
content:
Happy New Year. It is January 1st, 2023, and the first day of 2023 mentorship. First things first, a little bit of
housekeeping. If you're new or just beginning under my wing as a student of Smart Money Concepts, this
is my YouTube channel. One of the questions that keeps coming up by new subscribers or casual viewers
is where to begin. There's a lot of videos here, so let's take a look at what my opinion is.

section_title: RECOMMENDED LEARNING PATH FOR FOREX TRADERS
content:
Navigate to the playlist button. If you are a Forex Trader or a Forex Trader in the making, this is where you
want to start:
- Market Maker Primer Course
- Market Maker Series
- Pattern Recognition Series
After completing those, proceed with:
- Geometry High Probability Scalping
- Scout Sniper Basic Field Guide
Then, begin the Core Content Lessons, starting from Month One and progressing through Month Seven to
the last one.

section_title: 2023 MENTORSHIP FOCUS AND LIVE SESSIONS
content:
If you're interested in the core focus for this year, which is primarily Index Futures Trading, I will be
providing daily commentary, Monday through Friday, subject to personal schedule conflicts or holidays.
For Forex, I will cover:
- Dollar Index
- Euro Dollar
- British Pound
For Index Futures, I will cover ES and NQ.
Every day, my macro view and daily chart bias will be shared from my perspective. All intraday
engagements will be on Index Futures.
I will conduct two live streams per week, reviewing real-time price feeds. This is for studying real-time price
delivery and algorithmic behavior. It is not a signal service; I will not trade live, but I will call the market live
on a one and five-minute basis to demonstrate the high degree of precision in algorithmic price delivery.
You will see this repeating.

section_title: LIVE SESSION STRUCTURE AND TWITTER AS BACKUP SYSTEM
content:
The live sessions will be up to two hours each, totaling four hours of 'crunch time' per week. The full videos
will be uploaded for those unable to attend live. This promises a 'full plate of ICT goodness' throughout
2023.
The second important point is to subscribe to my Twitter account, even if you typically avoid it. It will serve
as a backup system. While YouTube live streams will occur twice a week, their schedule will vary based on
the economic calendar, which will become clearer in February.

section_title: OFFICIAL TWITTER ACCOUNT AND ICT'S POLICY ON PAID CONTENT
content:
To access my official Twitter account, go to my YouTube channel and click 'Official ICT Twitter' in the upper
right-hand corner. Verify it's the correct account by checking the pinned tweet from October 5th, 2022, at
7:49 a.m.
Important:
- I will not invite you to Telegram, Instagram, or Discord channels.
- I do not use those platforms.
- I will never DM you, and you cannot DM me.
Everything you learn this year is free; there is no upsell or future mentorship planned. The only future paid
content will be four books: one fiction and three educational. These books are a final 'Soiree' in paid
education, marking a definitive end to that aspect of my offerings.

section_title: DEMONSTRATING LIVE TRADING PROOF
content:
Returning to Twitter later. Regarding videos, I recently uploaded two. One was a quick, sped-up rendition of
my PM session trade on ES from Friday, the last trading day of the year. This format is typically favored for
its brevity. The second upload was a real-time, unspeed-up version, specifically in response to requests to
confirm live execution versus post-recording. I set its playback at 0.25 speed to clearly demonstrate that the
system time matched the real-time market data as I closed TradingView, proving the live nature of the
recording.

section_title: VERIFYING REAL-TIME DATA EXECUTION
content:
If you observe the lower right-hand corner of the screen, you will notice that the computer's system time
matches the real-time data timestamp exactly as TradingView is closed. This specific example shows the
time as 4:02 PM on December 30th. This confirms that all demonstrated activity was executed live using
real-time data via TradingView.

section_title: PRE-MARKET ANALYSIS ON TWITTER AND CHART WALKTHROUGH
content:
On my YouTube profile, navigate to the video titled 'The Final Trade of 2022 in Real Time TradingView
Platform.' In the video description, you'll find a link to a Twitter thread I started and shared on Friday
morning, December 30th, 2022, at 7:41 AM New York time. I even corrected a mistake there regarding the
contract month at 7:42 AM. In this thread, I walk through the charts, including the daily and four-hour
charts, outlining Thursday's high and low and the relevant ranges. I provide a detailed pre-market analysis,
indicating the specific levels I was observing for that trading day.

section_title: TWITTER AS A PRIMARY ALERT SYSTEM
content:
At 8:20 AM, I highlighted specific levels, including a 'bicycle depot,' that the market was likely to be drawn
into. These annotations will make more sense upon reviewing the video. Following this, it's crucial to
subscribe to my Twitter. While I've been very active on Twitter in the past, in 2023, I will primarily use it for
critical alerts to draw your attention to important market events or information, especially outside of live
sessions.

section_title: ESSENTIAL TWITTER NOTIFICATIONS FOR UPDATES AND SCHEDULE CHANGES
content:
On live session days, I will not be tweeting extensively. Twitter Spaces, which were like podcasts, will no
longer occur; the last one was yesterday. Therefore, it's essential to enable Twitter notifications. Any alert I
issue on Twitter will be highly relevant to a real market event or a schedule change. For instance, if a
YouTube live session is scheduled and something unforeseen happens in my personal life, like an
emergency, you will receive a direct tweet informing you of the situation and any necessary actions. While
subscription isn't mandatory, you cannot complain about not knowing updates if you're not following the

section_title: TRADINGVIEW CHART SETUP AND ANNOTATION PROCESS
content:
Let's move to the chart. This is what the chart appears like when opened in TradingView, which is my
working interface. To maximize the view during recordings, I click a specific button to reveal my toolbar at
the bottom, then maximize the chart. This setup allows me to easily access my 'favorite list' of tools, which
is not extensive, for adding all necessary annotations. After the recording and pre-markup, the chart will
display all the elements used, showing how it looks after the analysis.

section_title: FORECASTING AN "INSIDE DAY" ON THE LAST TRADING DAY
content:
We discussed the high likelihood of Friday being an 'inside day.' This prediction, which I tweeted
beforehand, was based on it being the last trading day of the year. On such days, significant market moves,
either up or down, are improbable. Institutional funds and capital typically remain on the sidelines or are
squaring positions.
Considering December's bearish trend and the absence of a 'Santa Claus rally,' the market was expected
to consolidate, which it did within a small range. I highlighted Thursday's high and low, anticipating Friday's
trading to remain within that range, thus forming an inside day. Indeed, Friday saw a lower high than
Thursday's high and a higher low than Thursday's low, confirming the narrative.

section_title: LIQUIDITY AND INEFFICIENCIES ON THE FOUR-HOUR CHART
content:
Dropping down to a four-hour chart, I will maximize and add annotations. The focus is on Thursday's lows
and highs.
- Below all lows represents South Side liquidity (sell stops).
- Above old highs represents Buy Side liquidity (buy stops). This constitutes liquidity.
When analyzing a range, such as from one candle's high to another's low, if you're not looking at old highs
and lows for liquidity, you're looking for inefficiencies. For example, if only one candle passes between a
candle's low and another's high, this signifies a 'buy side imbalance, sell side inefficiency.' This is distinct
from a 'liquidity void.'

section_title: UNDERSTANDING LIQUIDITY VOIDS AND BALANCED PRICE RANGES
content:
A 'liquidity void' is an actual gap between a candle's high and another candle's low. Observe how the
market moved above a short-term high, specifically targeting and taking liquidity from that liquidity void.
This occurs because, between the high of one candle and the low of another, if only a single candle passed
through, in this case downwards, the market must later traverse it to achieve balance. It first comes up
halfway, which is 'consequent encroachment,' and then completes a full return. So, we see a pass-through
down, then a pass-up. This area then becomes a 'balanced price range' once the market has left it.

section_title: NAVIGATING INEFFICIENCIES ACROSS TIMEFRAMES AND THE COMMITMENT
content:
REQUIRED
Within this four-hour candle, we will later revisit these levels on a lower time frame. For clarity on lower time
frames, these levels will be marked with a heavy dashed line. There are also inefficiencies within this area
that are more visible on lower time frames, as demonstrated in my Friday morning Twitter walkthrough.
I understand this may feel complicated initially, but achieving highly precise trade executions and analysis
requires dedication. Embrace the fact that we will be together throughout 2023, and you will gain this
understanding. However, consistent daily attendance and note-taking are essential.

section_title: HOURLY CHART ANALYSIS
content:
- NARRATIVE VS. BIAS
Now, let's examine the hourly chart. We focus on Thursday's low and its associated liquidity pool (sell-side
liquidity) and Thursday's high (buy-side liquidity). My prediction for an 'inside day' on Friday, prior to the
8:30 and 9:30 AM market openings, was based on price staying within Thursday's high and low. Indeed,
price did not go lower, nor did it go higher than these respective points.
This concept is a 'narrative,' not 'bias.'
- 'Bias' refers to the direction you are trading.
- 'Narrative' describes how the day is likely to trade and what it will aim for.
A narrative is a game plan, a roadmap for market behavior, not a directional preference.

section_title: ROADMAPS AND EVENT SEQUENCES
content:
A 'narrative' is akin to knowing multiple routes for a journey based on experience, allowing you to articulate
the path from Point A to Point B. In the market, a narrative describes the likely drive towards liquidity,
whether higher or lower, based on a sequence of events.
Examples of narratives:
- Taking out a previous day's low to then run a previous day's high (an extreme, but possible).
- In low volatility, staying within the previous day's range.
- On a Monday, anticipating an 'inside day' refers to the previous Friday's range, as indices cannot be
traded over the weekend.
Understanding these narratives, and how price is likely to deliver, is complex and will require time and
study throughout 2023.

section_title: ADAPTING TO MARKET DYNAMICS AND ALGORITHMIC BEHAVIOR
content:
I do not always know the exact market narrative. Sometimes, I enter with an expectation, but I must adjust
and adapt to the market's behavior, as manual intervention can disrupt the anticipated algorithmic delivery.
This adaptability doesn't imply a lack of ability to find alternative trades, but rather acknowledges that the
market is a 'living, breathing organism' that makes decisions based on its operators, not merely buying and
selling pressure.
Now, transitioning to a 15-minute time frame, we can observe the signatures of price movement, where it
runs up, leaves, and then returns. We will delve into this further.

section_title: REVIEWING LIQUIDITY VOIDS AND IMBALANCES ACROSS TIMEFRAMES
content:
For new students finding this complex, I assure you that reviewing the 41 videos from Mentorship 2022 will
provide foundational understanding.
Here, we observe a genuine 'liquidity void.' The market initially leaves it, then returns to 'hit' it but cannot re
- enter because it has become a 'balanced price range.' Subsequently, the market moves and drifts lower,
revealing various imbalances.
Specifically, on the hourly chart, the range between a particular candle's high and another's low represents
a 'buy-side imbalance, sell-side inefficiency.' We will now examine this same area on the 15-minute chart.

section_title: VISUALIZING IMBALANCES AND CHARTING PHILOSOPHY
content:
On the 15-minute chart, this highlights the same price range, but with more candles visible. I'm focusing on
the range between a specific candle's high and another's low, marked in contrasting blue colors.
When I personally trade, I don't use many on-chart indicators; I 'see' the levels and work with a 'naked'
chart. This approach helps me focus without distraction. However, for new students, it's essential to
visualize these elements on the chart to grasp the precision and algorithmic response. I will elaborate on
this further.
We observe a 'buy-side imbalance, sell-side inefficiency' on this 15-minute chart. A similar, smaller yellow
- highlighted imbalance will become visible when we transition to the five-minute chart.

section_title: NESTED PD ARRAYS AND MULTI-TIMEFRAME IMBALANCE ANALYSIS
content:
On the five-minute chart, we identify a 'fair value gap' (FVG), highlighted by a yellow range between a
specific candle's high and low. This demonstrates a nesting of 'PD Arrays' (Fair Value Gaps) across
multiple timeframes:
- A larger FVG on the four-hour chart.
- An FVG on the hourly chart, defined by trend lines.
- An FVG on the 15-minute chart.
- And now, this FVG on the five-minute chart.
Reducing our focus to the five-minute chart, we also note relative equal highs which will align with the one
- minute chart.
Earlier, price left a 'balanced price range' with a 'liquidity void.' It then broke a low, attempted to return but
could not re-enter, leaving a small gap between a candle's low and high open. Subsequently, price moved
lower, penetrating into the hourly imbalance, then deeper into the 15-minute imbalance, and finally into the
five-minute imbalance at that specific point.

section_title: FIVE-HANDLE MOVE AND PRE-MARKET TARGET IDENTIFICATION
content:
You would then have witnessed my recording of a five-handle move, utilizing the volume imbalance, which
will be further detailed on the one-minute chart. This recording was shared on Twitter either yesterday or
Friday morning.
Subsequently, the market dived lower into an old order flow, becoming 'sloppy.' My attention was directed to
3864, a level I highlighted on Twitter Friday morning, stating to 'note 3864 at relative equal highs.' This
target was also included in the pre-market charts before significant market movement began.

section_title: ALGORITHMIC PRECISION IN PRICE ACTION
content:
The market precisely traded down to the low end of the four-hour imbalance. It briefly moved slightly
outside, then perfectly 'hit' the designated low. This demonstrates extreme precision. Subsequently, the
market rallied, returning into the hourly, 15-minute, and five-minute imbalances, extending these ranges
later in the day.

section_title: ALWAYS NEW YORK TIME
content:
If we annotate the 3 o'clock hour (15:00) with a vertical line, it's crucial to address a foundational point:
when using TradingView, regardless of your global location, always set your time settings to New York time.
Failure to do so will result in misalignment with the algorithm and my analysis, making it difficult to follow
the chart examples. This is non-negotiable, as markets operate on New York time.
For Index Futures, consistently use New York local time, irrespective of Daylight Saving Time. While not an
issue for Forex, for stock indices, all timings will be relative to New York. This clarifies the proper settings for
'kill zones' and operating hours.

section_title: ONE-MINUTE CHART AND PM SESSION TRADING STRATEGY
content:
Transitioning to a one-minute chart, we focus on the PM session, specifically the 3:00 to 4:00 PM window.
This offers a small, easy trading opportunity for those with limited time, aiming for five handles or more. A
'five-handle' move, for example, is from 36.00 to 31.00. While it might not initially seem easy, with proper
understanding, finding such moves within the market's fluctuations becomes very achievable.
So, what specifically are we looking for within this window?

section_title: CONFIRMING PRE-ANNOUNCED LIQUIDITY TARGETS ON TWITTER
content:
At 3 o'clock, we had a previously identified buy-side liquidity pool at 3864. This level was publicly
announced in the morning session, before noon New York time. I encourage you to verify this by checking
my Twitter feed from Friday, reviewing the time and date stamps. I never delete tweets, even mistakes. If I
err, students often correct me, and I acknowledge their understanding, or I issue a correctional tweet, as I
did on Friday morning regarding an incorrect contract month (e.g., calling out 'esh2022' instead of the
current front-month 'esh2023' for Index Futures ES).
POST-3

section_title: TARGETING LIQUIDITY AND SHORT-COVERING RALLIES
content:
If you watch this video later, you can still reference my past tweets and charts. The recordings also
demonstrate everything. After 3 o'clock, I look for confirmation of a move towards 3864. This area aligns
with a five-minute fair value gap, which I will now highlight.
The price movement began within the upper portion of the four-hour range, specifically above its midpoint.
This is not a premium entry in the traditional sense because we are in the final hour of trading, and the
expectation is a run above 3864. This target is significant because there's buy-side liquidity resting above it,
and a short-covering rally is highly probable.

section_title: ALGORITHMIC PRICE RALLIES VS. TRADITIONAL BELIEFS
content:
My perspective on market rallies often diverges from others. Many traders have differing opinions on
market mechanics, price action, and consolidation.
The market rallies because the algorithm continuously offers higher prices. Market orders are filled at these
rising prices. As long as the algorithm continues 'spooling' (offering successively higher prices without
allowing lower ones), it will force prices upward. The volume of short entries becomes irrelevant; if the
algorithm is programmed to go higher, it will, squeezing out short positions.

section_title: ALGORITHMIC REPRICING
content:
When a trader's narrative or pattern fails, they often attribute it to 'short covering' or 'market squeezes.'
However, the reality is the algorithm is simply repricing higher. As a default consequence, participants
covering their short positions are forced to buy back. Traders who are short, especially on the last day of
the year when they want to lock in profits and realize capital gains for tax purposes, will re-evaluate holding
their positions as price relentlessly grinds higher. This sustained upward movement, driven by the
algorithm, was precisely what I expected and outlined in my Friday morning recording.

section_title: ALGORITHMIC DRIVE TO LIQUIDITY POOLS
content:
Revisit the video with this insight to fully grasp the explanation. The market's upward movement is not
driven by general buying pressure. Instead, the 'pressure' comes from the liquidity pool resting above 3864,
where a collective of buy stops from short positions are located. The algorithm, through its coding,
recognizes this old 'relative equal high' (two old highs, as I showed on Twitter) as a reference point. It
cannot 'see' individual orders but knows this is where orders are concentrated. The objective is to
implement a 'buy model' that pushes price higher, forcing short sellers to cover. The algorithm exclusively
ticks higher, with any downward movements being muted and small. The magnitude and velocity of price
action are predominantly on the up-close (green candles), gravitating directly towards the liquidity pool
above 3864.

section_title: YEAR-END LIQUIDITY AND PRECISION TRADING SETUP
content:
We anticipate this scenario because it's the last trading day of the year, motivating traders to close
positions and realize profits. The directional 'bias' of the analysis points directly to this liquidity pool.
Observe the price movement: it initially moved outside the four-hour imbalance, then precisely touched the
designated low. This level of precision is perfect and cannot be improved upon. This occurred during the
lead-up to the final hour of trading.
Following this, the market moved as expected. You don't have to be an early buyer; instead, look for the
'easy setup' where everything aligns perfectly.

section_title: MARKET RALLY, IMBALANCE FORMATION, AND VIDEO LENGTH PHILOSOPHY
content:
The market rallies, creating a smaller imbalance at this point. I'm now removing the 15-minute chart for
clearer visualization of this area. In your own charting, remember these elements. This tutorial aims to
guide your mindset and provide comprehensive insights, even if it means extending the video beyond my
preference for concise 2023 content. I lengthen videos only when necessary to ensure accurate
understanding. So, let's remove the four-hour range and then the 15-minute chart.

section_title: ONE-MINUTE FAIR VALUE GAP ANALYSIS
content:
With only the one-minute chart remaining, we observe a 'buy-side imbalance, sell-side inefficiency.' Let's
examine specific candle data for precision.
- The high of this candle is 3839.25.
- The low of this candle is exactly 3839.25. It neither went one tick below nor fell short; it hit it perfectly.
- Similarly, the low of this other candle is 3839.25.
This level of precision is absolutely perfect and undeniable. I outlined it in real-time, it was recorded,
executed upon, and you witnessed its delivery. Such precise price action would not occur without an
underlying algorithm.

section_title: STOP LOSS PLACEMENT AND FAIR VALUE GAP LOGIC
content:
My stop loss was strategically placed just below the opening of a specific candle, anticipating that the
market would respect that one-minute fair value gap. This is because we had already interacted with the
hourly fair value gap (its low and high). I observed price descend, touch the top of this hourly FVG, and
then I initiated my first buy at that point. I subsequently added more to the position, and then again, to
demonstrate conviction that the stop would not be hit. The logic hinges on the 60-minute fair value gap now
providing support, as the one-minute gap had already closed. Time was also a factor; it was approximately
3:25 PM (15:25 New York time), signaling the session was advancing.

section_title: ANTICIPATING ALGORITHMIC BREAKAWAY GAPS AND LIQUIDITY DRIVES
content:
At this point, the market is compelled to gravitate towards the liquidity above. Short sellers are incentivized
to cover their positions as the algorithm continuously reprices higher.
I identify the 'kick-off' point when candles demonstrate an unwillingness to close at the low end of the 60
- minute fair value gap. Once this is observed, I anticipate the market to leave this area and accelerate
upward. This specific candle marks that breakout, initiating significant acceleration.
As it breaks away, it forms another fair value gap, which is characteristic of a 'breakaway gap' and is likely
to remain open. This means, unlike other gaps, it's not expected to be filled by price returning.

section_title: BREAKAWAY GAPS AND CONTINUATION
content:
Consider the gap between a specific candle's high and low, which I've highlighted with a rectangle. The
market completely fills this gap perfectly, repeatedly. This is not about support, resistance, Wyckoff, or
supply/demand; it is purely the algorithm at work. Once it fills this gap, it then leaves it.
Crucially, as it leaves this filled gap, it initiates another fair value gap. This indicates a 'consolidation
- expansion-continuation' pattern, not a 'consolidation-expansion-reversal.' The algorithm will not
consolidate, expand, then immediately retrace. Instead, it will leave this newly formed gap open. I provided
two examples this week, identifying this behavior before it was confirmed to the viewer that price would not
return to fill or overlap this range. Specifically, the range of an up-candle, between its open and a
subsequent candle's low, will not be revisited.

section_title: BALANCED PRICE RANGES AND STOP LOSS MANAGEMENT
content:
When a single candle is overlapped, and price is moving upward, it must go down to rebalance. We have
observed both buy-side and sell-side delivery. Once price leaves that range and moves above a specific
candle's low, it becomes a 'balanced price range' and is unlikely to revisit that area, especially if a single
candle aggressively passes through it.
If a candle closes, and the subsequent candle begins forming, I then know a gap exists that will remain
open. However, if price starts to re-enter this low area, I would buy, confident it won't go lower. My stop loss
would be placed just below the bodies of these candles, which is where it moved next after I trailed it up.

section_title: OBSERVING PRICE GRAVITATION AND LIQUIDITY TARGETS
content:
I would then observe the candles, watching them gravitate towards the liquidity pool at 38.53.5. Once this
was filled, the market gravitated to a short-term buy-side high. At this point, the previous hourly range is no
longer relevant as price has left it. I'm now adding a rectangle back to the chart to properly illustrate the
execution. Here we see the five-minute fair value gap.

section_title: TRADING BREAKAWAY GAPS AND BREAKERS
content:
This is a 'Breakaway Gap,' a type of fair value gap that is unlikely and unwanted to be traded back into. By
temporarily removing the five-minute chart, we gain a new dynamic perspective on my execution. I am
buying at the top of the fair value gap as price enters it. I then continue to buy as it tags the fair value gap
on the one-minute chart, timing entries perfectly. Additionally, I add another position because this area also
functions as a 'breaker.'

section_title: BREAKERS AND BALANCED PRICE RANGES
content:
I'll explain the secondary entry. By adding a rectangle to this up-candle, you can see I'm buying inside it.
This action targets a short-term low, going after its liquidity and causing a rebalance. Once price runs up,
this area becomes a 'balanced price range' and should not be re-entered. The subsequent single candle
passing through on the outside forms a 'breakaway gap.'
This advanced concept is not found in retail trading books. I'm deliberately withholding some details for my
upcoming books, aware that others are already publishing content based on my teachings without
attribution. Rest assured, I will address such instances.

section_title: EXECUTING ON ALGORITHMIC LIQUIDITY TARGETS
content:
As price ran up into the buy-side liquidity, it hit the target precisely. All entries were made within the final
hour of trading. Although the liquidity target was met roughly two minutes later than my ideal four o'clock
hour expectation (a 'miss' by my high standards, attributed to human factor), it demonstrates the pursuit of
extreme precision.
Our approach is to identify algorithmic opportunities where price will seek liquidity, rather than relying on
patterns, harmonics, or trendline analysis. This is about understanding the raw market's algorithmic
behavior, which consistently repeats.

section_title: REVERSING VS. STAYING OUT FOR NEW AND FUNDED TRADERS
content:
The algorithm repeats at specific times, but at other times, the market's expected behavior may prompt a
decision to reverse or stay out.
For new traders, it is better to 'err on the side of caution' by staying out and observing, rather than reversing
a position. While experienced traders sometimes, but not always, find opportunities to reverse if their initial
analysis doesn't materialize, new traders should avoid this. Reversing trades prematurely is a common way
to blow an account.
If you are a funded trader, particularly through prop firms, never reverse. Instead, stay out and wait for the
next trading session.

section_title: SESSION MANAGEMENT AND AVOIDING MARKET DECEPTIONS
content:
If you trade the morning session (8:30 to 11:00 AM New York time) and incur a loss or your analysis proves
incorrect, do not reverse. Instead, cease trading for the morning and wait for the afternoon session.
Oftentimes, if your morning analysis goes awry, the PM session will offer clearer, cleaner setups.
The market frequently employs 'broken wing tricks,' similar to a robin feigning injury to lure a predator away
from its nest. It creates seemingly easy opportunities to trap unsuspecting traders. As a novice, avoid falling
victim to these market deceptions. Do not chase what appears to be easy prey by reversing your position.
This will lead to losses.

section_title: PM SESSION TRADING, MARKET SIMPLICITY, AND EXPERIENCE-BASED DECISIONS
content:
As a new trader, avoid falling victim to market tricks. If your initial trade fails, don't reverse and chase
another opportunity; instead, wait for the next trading session.
For the PM session (2:00 to 4:00 PM New York time), especially the last hour, you will typically find a
significant move. This move will be a run to an old high, an old low, or an imbalance. This isn't complicated.
Many critics don't truly understand my methodology, despite watching videos. My detailed explanations
stem from extensive experience. The key is not merely knowing when to buy or sell, or how specific
candles behave, but understanding the nuanced 'layering of experience on circumstances' that dictates
market action. What people often mistake for liquidity voids are, in fact, not.

section_title: ICT'S AUTHORITY ON SMART MONEY CONCEPTS AND ALGORITHMIC TRADING
content:
As demonstrated, I clearly know what I'm looking for, execute on it, and explain it beforehand. The charts
show precise market reactions to the levels I identify, proving the existence of an algorithm. I possess an
unparalleled understanding of this algorithm, and I am continuously refining it. No one else has the
authority to teach 'Smart Money Concepts' because I authored them; these are my unique codings and
market signatures. Therefore, it is foolish and harmful for others to write books, create courses, or offer
mentorships attempting to teach these concepts without a complete understanding, simply because they
do not truly know them.

section_title: STUDENT RESPONSIBILITY AND AVOIDING COUNTER-ALGORITHMIC TRADING
content:
This year, I am making myself available to all of you, but it is your diligent responsibility to show up daily. If
you choose not to follow my instructions, or if you attempt to bend the rules and logic to create your 'own
thing,' you will inevitably fail. It's that simple. During market moves like these, you will observe others on live
streams that they're attempting to sell short, precisely because they lack the knowledge to trade such
conditions. They are acting against the underlying order flow and the very algorithm they believe they are
trading with, when in reality, they are not.

section_title: FOCUS ON FIVE AND ONE-MINUTE CHARTS
content:
Your role is to simply observe, take notes, and absorb the insights. During live sessions, I will not speak
extensively as I am doing now (this preamble is to set expectations). I will only speak when it is salient. We
will primarily focus on the five-minute and one-minute charts. I will annotate everything in a top-down
manner, ensuring all key elements are visible for clear understanding of my observations.
My screen will be split, displaying both a five-minute and a one-minute chart. We will not need to consult 15
- minute, hourly, four-hour, or daily charts, as all necessary reference points will be pre-marked on the five
- minute and one-minute charts. This setup will maintain your focus on the critical real-time market activity.

section_title: ICT'S STANCE ON LIVE TRADING, COMPLIANCE, AND AVOIDING MANIPULATION
content:
For every candle, I will highlight useful insights. I will not execute live trades during sessions, nor will I press
buttons to buy or sell, to avoid enticing anyone to trade. I do not wish to bear that responsibility, nor do I
engage in 'front-running,' 'pumping and dumping,' or influencing market action for personal gain. Due to the
channel's growing audience, I must prevent any accusations of manipulation (e.g., entering a trade, then
telling viewers to buy, only to exit after they've entered). Such actions are fraudulent, and I strictly avoid
them. This is why I've always taught using demo accounts and paper trading. However, this year, I
demonstrated a 100% return in five weeks in a live account, proving the efficacy of the methods. You don't
need to be perfect to succeed.

section_title: REALISTIC TRADING GOALS AND MANAGING EXPECTATIONS
content:
You do not need to trade every day or be involved in every single market move. Avoid the trap of comparing
your progress to social media or even my own executions. Viewing my trades as a benchmark for your
immediate skill level is unrealistic and fosters an unhealthy mindset. Instead, focus on consistently finding
and executing five-handle moves. If you can achieve this a few times a week, you can do exceptionally well,
even trading just one Mini contract on ES.
For example, an entry at 3847, rising to 3848, yields a $50 profit. In a past trade, entering at 3847 saw a
maximum drawdown of only one point, with the candle low reaching 3846. I confirmed my recollection,
noting the one-point drawdown.

section_title: DEEPER UNDERSTANDING OF BREAKERS AND ORDER BLOCK THEORY
content:
Using a 'breaker' involves a low, a high, a lower low, and then price leaving it. When referring to price
'leaving,' I'm often referencing the high of a specific candle body, not just the candle range. This highlights a
nuance in 'order block theory' that will be fully elaborated in my upcoming book. This book, though priced,
is intended as a final 'gift' to the community, serving to distinguish my authentic concepts from the 'garbage'
being published by others who misunderstand or misrepresent my teachings. I intentionally withheld certain
details in the past, anticipating others would attempt to repackage and claim my unique concepts as their
own, simply by renaming them without proper attribution or understanding.

section_title: THE UNIQUENESS OF ICT'S TRANSPARENT TRADING METHODOLOGY
content:
This year, 2023, is your opportunity to apply what I teach, because I guarantee no one else will
demonstrate trading with my level of precision and consistency. This is a testament to the fact that signal
service providers, who should be required to show a verifiable track record, often fall short. They might
present MT4 trade lists, but they fail to publicly engage the market, use real stop losses, demonstrate trade
management, and articulate the underlying logic of their methodology. I believe I am unique in consistently
providing this complete transparency.

section_title: ACCOUNT TRADING
content:
While critics might point out that I demonstrate trading in a paper trading account on TradingView, they are
correct. However, one can only trade live data on TradingView. I operate this way for compliance reasons,
as I am not a licensed financial advisor. Thus, all my discussions on price action are presented within the
context of paper trading and demo environments.
An astute student will eventually recognize that these methods consistently work with real-time data. The
crucial factors are consistency, precision, and the repeatable logic, not whether it's a live account. You
witnessed this in 2022, and in 2023, you will see it demonstrated in 4K resolution.

section_title: A PERSONAL AND DISCIPLINED DECISION
content:
Eventually, you will decide whether to transition from demo or paper trading to live trading. This is a
personal decision, and I will never instruct my students to do it. In fact, I often advise against it because,
90% of the time, traders are not truly ready. You might feel ready due to impulsivity, desiring quick profits as
a solution to deeper issues like depression or dissatisfaction. However, true readiness means being driven
by discipline, knowing precisely when to act and, more importantly, when not to.

section_title: OPTIMAL TRADING WINDOWS AND IDENTIFYING SETUPS
content:
Examining the chart, particularly the highlighted time frame: The session officially closes at 4:15 PM, with a
'bleed over' as the closing bell occurs then.
The last hour and 15 minutes of trading are crucial. Your trade setup should ideally form in the last hour,
between 3:00 PM and 4:00 PM New York time. If you haven't identified a setup by 10 minutes before the
hour, it's best to refrain from trading and simply observe.
Generally, a 'sweet spot' for finding opportunities is between 3:15 PM and 3:45 PM (15:15 to 15:45 in
TradingView, New York time). During this window, I specifically look for liquidity either above the market (if
bullish) or below (if bearish), or for the presence of an imbalance.

section_title: DEFINING IMBALANCES AND PRICE REBALANCING
content:
An 'imbalance' (specifically, a 'buy-side imbalance, sell-side inefficiency') acts as a magnet, drawing price
back into these areas for rebalancing, as demonstrated. Price will either target:
- Pools of liquidity above an old high or relative equal highs.
- Pools of liquidity below an old low or relative equal lows.
- An area it needs to revisit for repricing and rebalancing.
Crucially, simply entering an area does not mean it's 'rebalanced.' It's considered 'balanced' while price is
interacting within it, but it only becomes a 'balanced price range' after price leaves it. This distinction is a
key difference in my teaching compared to other educators, and I always provide evidence ('receipts') to
support these concepts.

section_title: CONCLUDING REMARKS AND UPCOMING MENTORSHIP SCHEDULE
content:
I hope this was insightful. This is the only installment for this week; I'll share something next week, building
towards a more consistent pattern of real-time analysis and markups starting February 7th, 2023. Until
then, there will be one video per week, serving as a warm-up.
Between now and February 7th, you should:
- Watch the playlists I recommended at the beginning of this video.
- Subscribe to my Twitter. I won't tweet excessively; it's exclusively for drawing your attention to important
updates.
It's crucial to have Twitter alerts and notifications enabled, as I use it to get your immediate attention in
ways YouTube community posts sometimes cannot. Twitter is like a direct text it will instantly deliver links
to charts, pictures, comments, or schedule changes. Your Twitter following is essential for staying current
with my thoughts, actions, and schedule, as I will only communicate scheduling through Twitter, not
YouTube.
Until next week, Happy New Year, and I look forward to teaching you amazing things in real-time.
